summary: This lecture from the Muslim Community Center of Tucson discusses the core purpose of Islamic existence through Quranic teachings. The speaker explains that belief in a single creator is not unique to Islam, as most religions and philosophies acknowledge one supreme being. The fundamental distinction of Islam is the requirement to worship Allah alone without partners and to reject all forms of intercession through saints, idols, or intermediaries. The speaker illustrates how the early Arabs of Quraysh, despite acknowledging Allah as the sole creator, maintained polytheistic practices by worshiping idols they believed would intercede with Allah on their behalf—a practice the Prophet Muhammad came to abolish. The lecture traces this concept of intercession-based idolatry throughout human history and even into modern religious practices, emphasizing that the true message of Islam centers on exclusive monotheistic worship (Tawheed). The second part of the lecture focuses on Allah's mercy and the perpetually open door of repentance, drawing from Quranic verses about forgiveness even for the gravest sins, and emphasizing that the Quran is fundamentally a book of hope for all of humanity.
suggested_title: Understanding Islamic Monotheism (Tawheed): Worship of Allah Alone and the Rejection of Intercession
